# Source Card: Validating CALIPER pediatric reference intervals in a U.S. population using retrospective outpatient data and RefineR

## Summary
This peer-reviewed validation study (Yale School of Medicine; Clin Chim Acta 2026;584:120846) tests whether Canadian CALIPER pediatric reference intervals transfer to a diverse US pediatric outpatient population, deriving local intervals from 51,863 patients under 19 years by two methods (RefineR indirect modeling and CLSI EP28-A3c). It establishes CALIPER as a validated external reference-interval source for US pediatric outpatients and shows RefineR aligns with CALIPER more often (86/88 groups) than CLSI (79/88). For the pediatric CDS module it supports adopting CALIPER-style age-/sex-partitioned intervals while flagging ALT, AST, ALP, and BUN as analytes where local preanalytical/physiological context matters — but it does NOT supply the per-analyte numeric cutoffs (those are in paywalled tables).

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) 51,863 US outpatients <19 y, single institution, Jan 2023–Dec 2024, 9 chemistry analytes — locator: Abstract Methods — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:9bf3fcac382590ded311de98a598898c8807b3e6b5bb02165c579e9a5c6ca668]"
- (ev_002) RefineR aligned with CALIPER in 86 of 88 groups — locator: Abstract Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:3918473b63bab41c1014b47bb701608109f36a3d5b94e960022f6cbeae8e3029]"
- (ev_003) CLSI EP28-A3c aligned with CALIPER in 79 of 88 (RefineR superior) — locator: Abstract Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:3df45edddd7f3e4ec35c3c8dd413a4d23402c8360953e33ad01a3892e22a4905]"
- (ev_004) Discrepancies concentrated in ALT/AST/ALP/BUN, driven by sample type, BMI, hydration — locator: Abstract Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:40901d2fe15c33fed186a58925c9ecca5350461e7865bba35734cbda32dd3e25]"
- (ev_005) Conclusion: CALIPER validated externally for US peds outpatients; RefineR practical for verifying external RI implementation — locator: Abstract Conclusion — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:4db8d18ea64e358204267929a9d7fa89f7e8092d8734983b9ab6ca3d2c5fb834]"
- (ev_006) Background: pediatric RIs are hard due to age-related physiological variation, small samples, preanalytical variability — locator: Abstract Background — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:742a5319acccbf53a03bc2f9b771c83390215110c64dcfb1ca466807f13964cc]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: US pediatric OUTPATIENTS under 19 years, both sexes, partitioned into 88 age- and gender-specific analyte groups; NOT neonatal/GA-PNA-specific (no gestational/postnatal-age banding described).
- Assay/method: Reference intervals are method-dependent; the study contrasts RefineR (indirect/mixed-data modeling) vs CLSI EP28-A3c, comparing both to CALIPER using 95% CI and total allowable error (TAE) flagging. The analyzer/instrument platform is NOT stated in the accessible abstract (a separate same-group conference abstract reported Roche Cobas, but that is a different item with a different N and analyte list — not asserted here).
- Lifecycle: Published 2026-01; CLSI comparator version is EP28-A3c. No retirement/superseding relationship stated.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- None. This source defines/validates reference-interval derivation methodology; it does not define red-flag, referral-trigger, or scope-exit thresholds, and issues no diagnosis/treatment/dosing directives.

## Limitations & conflicts
- GAP — per-analyte numeric reference-interval cutoff values (e.g., specific ALT/AST/ALP/BUN low–high limits by age/sex) are NOT locatable in the accessible source (paywalled tables behind ScienceDirect; no PMC full text). Do NOT assert any per-analyte numeric interval from this source; only the study-level counts (51,863; 86/88; 79/88) are verbatim-locatable.
- Conflict (preserved, not averaged) — RefineR vs CLSI EP28-A3c disagree on alignment count (86/88 vs 79/88); the paper states RefineR is superior for this outpatient setting. Recorded in trust.conflicts_with; do not collapse into a single "method-agnostic" figure.
- Conflict (preserved) — ALT, AST, ALP, and BUN diverge from CALIPER, attributed to preanalytical/physiological variables (sample type, BMI, hydration); local context may warrant institution-specific intervals for these analytes.
- Scope limits — single US institution, retrospective outpatient (indirect method, mixed population) rather than a healthy-reference direct study; external validity to other regions/populations is not established by this source.
- The Roche Cobas analyzer platform is associated with a SEPARATE conference abstract by the same group, not this paper's abstract — analyzer for this dataset is unconfirmed here.
